Żurek Śląski
Silesian Sour Rye Soup

Ingredients:
- 2 lbs pork bones or knuckle meat
- 4 cups water
- 2 cups rye sourdough starter liquid (żur)
- 1 large onion, quartered
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 carrots, sliced
- 2 parsnips, sliced
- 1 cup mushrooms, sliced
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 teaspoon caraway seeds
- 1 teaspoon marjoram
- 1/2 cup pearl barley (optional)
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 hard-boiled eggs, halved
- 1/4 cup fresh dill
- Sour cream for serving
- Rye bread for serving

Instructions:

Stock preparation:
Bring water to boil in large pot.
Add pork bones and onion.
Simmer 45 minutes, skimming foam.
Strain stock, discard bones and onion.

Soup:
Return stock to pot.
Add carrots, parsnips, mushrooms.
Add bay leaves, caraway seeds, marjoram.
Simmer 20 minutes until vegetables are tender.

Add pork meat if using boneless cut, shred into bite-sized pieces.
Pour in żur liquid slowly, stirring constantly.
Add barley if using, simmer additional 20 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Adjust sourness by adding more żur if desired.

Finish:
Add fresh dill just before serving.
Place hard-boiled egg halves in bowl.
Ladle hot soup over eggs.
Top with dollop of sour cream.
Serve with thick rye bread on the side.

Prep time: 30 minutes
Cooking time: 90 minutes
Serves: 6-8 people

Note: Żur (rye sourdough starter) can be made 3-5 days ahead
by fermenting water with crushed rye bread at room temperature.
